_note: Canonical world dataset for the map (regions, locations, terrain, routes, faction control, event seeds). Coordinates are approximate real-world lat/long; acceptable error ~10km. Cleaned to data only.

terrain_types.name: Salt Flats
terrain_types.description: Extremely flat, reflective salt crust with minimal cover and long sight lines.

terrain_types.name: Urban Ruins
terrain_types.description: Collapsed buildings, alleys and debris fields providing dense cover and complex sight lines.

terrain_types.name: Mountain Ridge
terrain_types.description: Steep, rocky high ground with excellent observation but difficult movement.

terrain_types.name: Desert Scrub
terrain_types.description: Sparse bushes, rocks and dry soil offering limited cover and moderate sight lines.

terrain_types.name: Forest Hills
terrain_types.description: Wooded hills and valleys with heavy vegetation and broken sight lines.

regions.name: New California Republic
regions.real_world_extent_hint: Southern California, parts of Nevada, Baja California, portions of Oregon.

terminal_entry.title: NCR STATE ARCHIVE – NEW CALIFORNIA REPUBLIC
body: Representative republic established from the settlement of Shady Sands, expanding across much of pre-war southern California and adjoining territories.
At peak it incorporates multiple contiguous states reaching north toward Oregon and south toward Baja, overlaying pre-war borders rather than preserving them.
The dominant organized power in the western wasteland, projecting law, trade and military presence into former US states without restoring their exact pre-war lines.

regions.name: Mojave Wasteland
regions.real_world_extent_hint: Southern Nevada and northwest Arizona around Las Vegas and Hoover Dam.

terminal_entry.title: MOJAVE SURVEY RECORD – MOJAVE WASTELAND
body: Arid desert and canyon lands centred on the pre-war Mojave Desert and Colorado River corridor, including the greater Las Vegas area and Hoover Dam.
Territorial divisions reflect NCR, Legion and independent holdings.
Control of Mojave transport routes and Hoover Dam output is crucial for NCR expansion and rival power projection into the interior.

regions.name: Capital Wasteland
regions.real_world_extent_hint: District of Columbia, southern Maryland, northern Virginia, adjacent Pennsylvania.

terminal_entry.title: FEDERAL EMERGENCY RECORD – CAPITAL WASTELAND
body: Devastated urban and suburban zone centred on the ruins of Washington, D.C., extending into surrounding portions of Virginia, Maryland and Pennsylvania.
Post-war inhabitants reference the area as a single Capital Wasteland rather than by pre-war state names.
Contains critical pre-war infrastructure — monuments, water projects and military sites — a focal point for Enclave and Brotherhood operations.

regions.name: Commonwealth
regions.real_world_extent_hint: Boston metropolitan area and central Massachusetts.

terminal_entry.title: COMMONWEALTH GOVERNANCE FILE – MASSACHUSETTS COMMONWEALTH
body: Post-war designation for the Boston and central Massachusetts area, where city and town identities persist but formal state structures have collapsed.
The term Commonwealth is widely used in place of the pre-war state name.
Contains Diamond City, the Institute and major ruins — central to human habitation and advanced technology in the northeast.

regions.name: Appalachia
regions.real_world_extent_hint: State of West Virginia and surrounding Appalachian valleys.

terminal_entry.title: US RECLAMATION BRIEF – APPALACHIA
body: Mountainous and forested territory corresponding to pre-war West Virginia, with scattered towns, mines and industrial sites.
Post-war references use Appalachia as a regional name rather than the former state boundary.
An early test case for post-war reclamation initiatives and automated aid systems.

regions.name: Midwestern Wasteland
regions.real_world_extent_hint: Kansas, Illinois and adjacent Midwestern states.

terminal_entry.title: BROTHERHOOD STRATEGIC BRIEF – MIDWESTERN WASTELAND
body: Central U.S. plains and urban centres across Kansas, Illinois and neighbouring states, marked by collapsed agriculture and dispersed communities.
Lacks cohesive post-war state structures — scattered towns, raider territories and emergent Brotherhood enclaves.
Offers access to surviving industrial corridors and transport routes; a priority for Brotherhood expansion.

regions.name: Wendover – Misfits Nuclear Wasteland
regions.real_world_extent_hint: Wendover, Utah and West Wendover, Nevada, on the Great Salt Flats.

terminal_entry.title: LOCAL SURVEY RECORD – WENDOVER SALT FLATS REGION
body: Post-war wasteland centred on the twin communities of Wendover, Utah and West Wendover, Nevada, on the ancient shores of Lake Bonneville beside the Toana Range.
Bleached salt flats offer minimal cover and harsh exposure; nearby ranges rise sharply from the desert floor.
A frontier theatre for NCR, Brotherhood and independent actors in the Misfits: Nuclear Wasteland scenario, with vault installations and outposts embedded in the local geography.

locations.name: Vault 13
locations.real_world_anchor: San Gabriel Mountains, northeast of Los Angeles, California

terminal_entry.title: Vault-Tec Secure Archive – VAULT 13
body: Standard Vault-Tec long-term habitation facility embedded in mountain rock strata northeast of the former Los Angeles metropolitan region.
Sealed at the Great War in 2077; population maintained under closed ecosystem protocols dependent on a single integrated water purification chip.
c.2161 — systemic water processing failure triggers dispatch of a surface surveyor, leading to extensive contact with external settlements.

locations.name: Shady Sands
locations.real_world_anchor: Desert region north-east of Los Angeles, California

terminal_entry.title: NCR CIVIC DATABASE – SHADY SANDS (FOUNDING NODE)
body: Agricultural and civic hub established by former Vault 15 residents in arid plains north-east of the old Los Angeles region.
c.2161 — a small but politically ambitious township experimenting with representative governance and codified law.
Later formalized as the capital of the New California Republic; origin point for NCR legal codes and expansion policy.

terminal_entry.title: COASTAL RUIN SURVEY – THE BONEYARD (LOS ANGELES)
body: A vast cemetery of collapsed skyscrapers and suburban sprawl corresponding to the former Los Angeles metropolitan area.
Fragmented communities and militias occupy districts such as Adytum.
A key source of salvage technology and printed knowledge, making control of habitable zones a recurring flashpoint.

terminal_entry.title: ORGANIZED CRIME DOSSIER – NEW RENO
body: Post-war entertainment and vice hub corresponding to pre-war Reno, dominated by competing crime families and casinos.
A major producer and distributor of chems, arms and illicit services for the western wasteland.
High opportunity for covert negotiation offset by extreme volatility and violent conflict.

terminal_entry.title: BROTHERHOOD FIELD LOG – CANYON
body: A small agricultural town on the former central-U.S. plains, used as a local recruitment and supply point.
Early Brotherhood Midwest engagements here establish a foothold against raider and mutant threats.
A staging ground for deeper incursions into the Midwestern Wasteland.

terminal_entry.title: CAPITAL WASTELAND TRAVEL NODE – MEGATON
body: A fortified shanty-town built around an undetonated atomic bomb in a former highway crater on the outskirts of ruined Washington.
Authority is exercised by a sheriff and council balancing a bomb-centric cult with merchant interests.
An early resupply and social hub for travellers entering the Capital Wasteland.

terminal_entry.title: MOJAVE SECURITY DOSSIER – NEW VEGAS STRIP
body: A fortified entertainment corridor corresponding to the pre-war Las Vegas Strip, restored under Mr. House and securitron police forces.
Neutral ground where NCR, Legion and independents compete via diplomacy, gambling and covert action.
Control of the Strip confers leverage over Hoover Dam negotiations and regional resource flows.

terminal_entry.title: COMMONWEALTH CIVIC RECORD – DIAMOND CITY
body: A major walled settlement built into and around the preserved structure of a pre-war baseball stadium.
Administered by a mayor and civic institutions balancing security with trade.
The symbolic and practical centre of human habitation in the Commonwealth.

terminal_entry.title: VAULT-TEC OPERATIONAL BRIEF – VAULT 76
body: A Control Vault with baseline social conditions, designated to open shortly after the Great War to spearhead reclamation of Appalachia.
Overseer directives focus on survey, resettlement and reactivation of surviving infrastructure.
The origin point for multiple reclamation initiatives and early regional governance experiments.

locations.name: Wendover Town Ruins
locations.real_world_anchor: Wendover, Utah, and West Wendover, Nevada

terminal_entry.title: LOCAL SETTLEMENT LOG – WENDOVER TOWN RUINS
body: Twin border towns straddling the line between former Utah and Nevada, reduced to scattered structures and half-functioning neon remnants along the salt flat edge.
Built on an ancient beach of Lake Bonneville and pressed against the rising Toana Range, the town offers limited vertical cover and long lines of sight across the flats.
A staging and trading nexus for wasteland travellers, linking vault installations, NCR patrols and independent scavenger camps.

locations.name: Great Salt Flats Expanse
locations.real_world_anchor: Bonneville Salt Flats east of Wendover, Utah

terminal_entry.title: ENVIRONMENTAL SURVEY – GREAT SALT FLATS
body: A bleached, featureless expanse of salt crust stretching east of Wendover, offering little cover and extreme visual exposure to any moving parties.
Sudden dust storms, mirage effects and reflective glare complicate navigation; vehicle travel leaves easy-to-track trails.
Frequently used as a kill-zone and ambush field; vault dwellers and NCR patrols cross it at considerable risk.

locations.name: Toana Range Overlook
locations.real_world_anchor: Foothills of the Toana Range south of Wendover, Utah

terminal_entry.title: TACTICAL SITE LOG – TOANA RANGE OVERLOOK
body: An elevated outpost and observation point carved into the lower slopes of the Toana Range, overlooking the town and salt flats below.
Provides long-range fields of fire and early warning against movements across the flats; bunkers and makeshift antennae dot the ridgeline.
Favoured by organized factions such as NCR units and Brotherhood detachments for coordinating operations in the Wendover theatre.

locations.name: Local Vault Installation
locations.real_world_anchor: Subsurface facility inferred beneath salt flats near Wendover, Utah

terminal_entry.title: VAULT-ADJACENT INCIDENT RECORD – WENDOVER INSTALLATION
body: An unofficial Vault-class installation embedded beneath the salt crust east of Wendover, a narrative anchor for vault dwellers in the scenario.
Access routes are concealed beneath maintenance shafts and collapsed pre-war test facilities; multiple factions contest interior control.
The primary origin point for vault dwellers entering the Wendover theatre.

connections.description: Primary caravan trail linking Shady Sands to the Hub across desert scrub and minor ruins.
Trade route descending from inland Hub corridors to the coastal ruins of the Boneyard.
Paved and dirt roads connecting the New Vegas Strip to the frontier town of Goodsprings.
Winding paths through Capital Wasteland ruins along the Potomac from Megaton to Rivet City.
Cracked highway leading east out of Wendover into the open salt flats.
Steep path from town edge up into the Toana Ridge outpost.
Concealed subsurface access from a maintenance block to the vault installation beneath the flats.

faction_zones.description: Core NCR territory around Shady Sands, Hub, and Boneyard.
NCR expansion into the Mojave, projecting power toward Hoover Dam and New Vegas.
Brotherhood presence in the Capital Wasteland, focused on technology recovery.
NCR outpost presence in Wendover town and mountain overlook.
Loose raider bands operating across the Great Salt Flats between Wendover and the vault.
Vault-origin inhabitants contesting interior control of the Wendover vault facility.

event_hooks.summary: Recurring ambushes of NCR caravans crossing the salt flats east of Wendover.
Attempted breach of the Wendover vault installation by raider forces, with NCR response.
Large-scale salvage operation into the Boneyard ruins for pre-war data and equipment.
